metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4863
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1: immunofluorescence and EM imaging show VAMP4 is a marker of recycling endosomes in soma and dendrites of hippocampal neurons.

Figure 2: shRNA knockdown of VAMP4, but not VAMP2, impairs recycling endosome exocytosis and recycling to the plasma membrane.

Figure 5: VAMP4 regulates the recycling of AMPA receptors and its availability for chemical-LTP

Authors conclude: "These results show that AMPAR exocytosis depends on VAMP2, and that VAMP4 is necessary for the trafficking of AMPARs to a compartment that undergoes exocytosis after cLTP.". Experimental description: M&M: "Dissociated hippocampal neurons from E18 Sprague-Dawley rat embryos of either sex were prepared as described (Kaech and Banker, 2006)."

Plasmids generated in this study have been deposited to Addgene (VAMP4-SEP #174406, VAMP4 KD1 mScarlet shRNA #174407, VAMP4 KD2 mScarlet shRNA #174408).
